Dietitian
Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN) Guide

Issued by the New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition under the NYSED Office of the Professions. Data verified for 2026.

$294
$115 Application + $179 Registration
Renewal
$179
Every 3 years

License Duration
3 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition

Albany, NY 12234-1000

Email

Credential Categories

New York Dietitian License Types

Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN)

The standard credential for practicing dietetics and nutrition in New York State. Demands completion of a Master's degree, ACEND-approved supervised practice, and passing the national CDR exam.

Application Process

How to Apply for a New York Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN)

Complete Education & Exam Requirements

Earn a Master's degree, complete at least 1,000 hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice, and pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

Take the Child Abuse Identification Course

New York State requires all healthcare professionals to complete state-approved coursework in identifying and reporting child abuse before applying for certification.

Apply Online via NYSED

Submit your application for a new CDN certification through the Office of the Professions online portal and pay the combined $294 application and initial registration fee.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official College TranscriptsMust be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the NYSED.
  • CDR Certification VerificationProof of active registration sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Child Abuse Training CertificateCertificate of completion for the mandatory NYS child abuse identification coursework.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

New York State does not issue temporary permits or provisional licenses for dietitians or nutritionists.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

New York does not maintain automatic reciprocity agreements with other states. Out-of-state applicants must apply for a new license by equivalence, providing direct verification of their out-of-state license, CDR registration, and proof they meet all New York education and experience standards.

Frequently Asked Questions

New York Dietitian FAQ

How much does a New York dietitian license cost?

The total initial cost is $294, which covers the NYSED's $115 application fee and the $179 fee for your first three-year registration period.

Do I need a master's degree to become a CDN in New York?

Yes. As of January 1, 2024, a minimum of a master's degree is required by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to sit for the national RDN exam, which serves as the exam basis for NY state certification.

Is continuing education required to renew a New York CDN license?

The NYSED does not mandate additional state-level continuing education for renewal, but you must maintain your active CDR registration, which requires completing 75 hours of continuing professional education every 5 years.
